[Bash] cd "C:\Users\sean\Projects\Salesforce\DigitalDreams\CaseOps" && git add force-app/main/default/objects/Opportunity/fields/Account_Billing_State__c.field-meta.xml force-app/main/default/objects/Opportunity/fields/Account_Main_Practitioner__c.field-meta.xml outputs/investigations/HEAL-33369.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF' Add Account_Billing_State and Account_Main_Practitioner formula fields to force-app  - Account_Billing_State__c: Formula field referencing Account.BillingState - Account_Main_Practitioner__c: Formula field referencing Account.Main_Practitioner__r.Name - Both fields match existing Account_Is_Compliante__c pattern (Formula Text type) - Verified fields populate correctly via SOQL in Sandbox - Ready for Production Gearset deployment to support HEAL-33369 time-to-schedule reports  Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com> EOF )"

CaseOps LLM: Claude Code CLI (CASEOPS_LLM_AUTH=claude_code; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY omitted).
Reading existing outputs and playbook to understand current state.
Reading workflow to understand pipeline steps for this stage.
Examining Sandbox for similar Opportunity fields and their permissions to apply to the new fields.
[Bash] sf sobject get --sobject Opportunity --target-org 10xhealth--sean --json | jq '.fields[] | select(.custom==true and .type=="string") | {name, label, custom, type, calculated}' | head -100
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata CustomObject:Opportunity --target-org 10xhealth--sean --output-dir /tmp/sf_retrieve 2>&1 | head -20
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata CustomObject:Opportunity --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir /tmp/sf_retrieve 2>&1 | tail -10
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata CustomObject:Opportunity --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./temp_retrieve 2>&1 | tail -5 && ls temp_retrieve/unpackaged/objects/Opportunity/
Need to check Sandbox permission sets for field-level security. Opening Sandbox to view permission set assignments.
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata PermissionSet --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./temp_perm_retrieve 2>&1 | tail -3
[Bash] ls temp_perm_retrieve/objects/PermissionSet/ 2>/dev/null | head -20 || find temp_perm_retrieve -name "*.permissionset-meta.xml" | head -20
Writing script to add field permissions for the two new Opportunity fields to all 25 permission sets.

[Bash] cat > /tmp/update_perms.py << 'EOF' import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET import os import glob  # List of permission sets that have Account_Is_Compliante__c perm_sets = [     "Accounting_Basic",     "Accounting_Manager",     "Admin_Team",     "Advisor_Assistant",     "Doctor_Staff",     "Executive",     "Marketing_Manager",     "Marketing_Staff",     "Medical_Coordinator",     "Medical_Staff",     "Nursing_Staff",     "Office_Manager",     "Office_Staff_Basic",     "Operations_Manager",     "Operations_Team",     "PSR_Team",     "Read_Only_Opportunity",     "sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms",     "Shipping_Coordinator",     "SteadyMD_Permission",     "System_Administrator",     "System_Administrator_API_Only",     "Wellness_Assistant",     "Wellness_Manager",     "Wellness_Team" ]  # Fields to add new_fields = [     "Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c",     "Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c" ]  # Register namespace ET.register_namespace('', 'http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata')  for pset in perm_sets:     filepath = f"force-app/main/default/permissionsets/{pset}.permissionset-meta.xml"          if not os.path.exists(filepath):         print(f"SKIP {pset}: file not found")         continue          tree = ET.parse(filepath)     root = tree.getroot()     ns = {'meta': 'http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata'}          # Find all existing field permissions     existing_fields = set()     for fp in root.findall('.//fieldPermissions', ns):         field_elem = fp.find('field', ns)         if field_elem is not None:             existing_fields.add(field_elem.text)          # Check if Account_Is_Compliante__c exists and get its structure     account_compliant_perm = None     for fp in root.findall('.//fieldPermissions', ns):         field_elem = fp.find('field', ns)         if field_elem is not None and field_elem.text == "Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c":             account_compliant_perm = fp             break          if not account_compliant_perm:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: Account_Is_Compliante__c not found")         continue          # Get the index where we should insert new fields (right after Account_Is_Compliante__c)     insert_index = None     for i, child in enumerate(root):         if child == account_compliant_perm:             insert_index = i + 1             break          if insert_index is None:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: could not find insertion point")         continue          # Add new field permissions if they don't already exist     fields_added = []     for new_field in new_fields:         if new_field not in existing_fields:             # Create new field permission element             new_fp = ET.Element('fieldPermissions')             editable = ET.SubElement(new_fp, 'editable')             editable.text = 'false'             field = ET.SubElement(new_fp, 'field')             field.text = new_field             readable = ET.SubElement(new_fp, 'readable')             readable.text = 'true'                          root.insert(insert_index, new_fp)             insert_index += 1             fields_added.append(new_field)          if fields_added:         tree.write(filepath, encoding='UTF-8', xml_declaration=True)         print(f"UPDATED {pset}: added {len(fields_added)} field(s)")     else:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: fields already present")  EOF python /tmp/update_perms.py

[Bash] cat > /tmp/update_perms2.py << 'EOF' import re import os  perm_sets = [     "Accounting_Basic", "Accounting_Manager", "Admin_Team", "Advisor_Assistant",     "Doctor_Staff", "Executive", "Marketing_Manager", "Marketing_Staff",     "Medical_Coordinator", "Medical_Staff", "Nursing_Staff", "Office_Manager",     "Office_Staff_Basic", "Operations_Manager", "Operations_Team", "PSR_Team",     "Read_Only_Opportunity", "sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms",     "Shipping_Coordinator", "SteadyMD_Permission", "System_Administrator",     "System_Administrator_API_Only", "Wellness_Assistant", "Wellness_Manager",     "Wellness_Team" ]  new_perms = """    <fieldPermissions>         <editable>false</editable>         <field>Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c</field>         <readable>true</readable>     </fieldPermissions>     <fieldPermissions>         <editable>false</editable>         <field>Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c</field>         <readable>true</readable>     </fieldPermissions>"""  for pset in perm_sets:     filepath = f"force-app/main/default/permissionsets/{pset}.permissionset-meta.xml"          if not os.path.exists(filepath):         print(f"SKIP {pset}: file not found")         continue          with open(filepath, 'r') as f:         content = f.read()          # Check if Account_Is_Compliante__c exists     if "Account_Is_Compliante__c" not in content:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: Account_Is_Compliante__c not found")         continue          # Check if new fields already exist     if "Account_Billing_State__c" in content and "Account_Main_Practitioner__c" in content:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: new fields already present")         continue          # Find the Account_Is_Compliante__c block and insert after it     pattern = r'(    <fieldPermissions>\s*<editable>false</editable>\s*<field>Opportunity\.Account_Is_Compliante__c</field>\s*<readable>true</readable>\s*</fieldPermissions>)'          if re.search(pattern, content):         # Add new perms after the Account_Is_Compliante__c block         new_content = re.sub(pattern, r'\1\n' + new_perms, content)         with open(filepath, 'w') as f:             f.write(new_content)         print(f"UPDATED {pset}")     else:         print(f"SKIP {pset}: could not find insertion pattern")  EOF python /tmp/update_perms2.py

[Bash] cat > /tmp/update_sfdc_accelerate.py << 'EOF' import re import os  filepath = "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms.permissionset-meta.xml"  new_perms = """    <fieldPermissions>         <editable>true</editable>         <field>Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c</field>         <readable>true</readable>     </fieldPermissions>     <fieldPermissions>         <editable>true</editable>         <field>Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c</field>         <readable>true</readable>     </fieldPermissions>"""  with open(filepath, 'r') as f:     content = f.read()  # Check if Account_Is_Compliante__c exists if "Account_Is_Compliante__c" not in content:     print(f"SKIP: Account_Is_Compliante__c not found") else:     # Check if new fields already exist     if "Account_Billing_State__c" in content and "Account_Main_Practitioner__c" in content:         print(f"SKIP: new fields already present")     else:         # Find the Account_Is_Compliante__c block (with editable=true)         pattern = r'(    <fieldPermissions>\s*<editable>true</editable>\s*<field>Opportunity\.Account_Is_Compliante__c</field>\s*<readable>true</readable>\s*</fieldPermissions>)'                  if re.search(pattern, content):             # Add new perms after the Account_Is_Compliante__c block             new_content = re.sub(pattern, r'\1\n' + new_perms, content)             with open(filepath, 'w') as f:                 f.write(new_content)             print(f"UPDATED sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms")         else:             print(f"SKIP: could not find insertion pattern")  EOF python /tmp/update_sfdc_accelerate.py
Verifying updated permission sets have correct format and deploying to Sandbox.
[Bash] sf project deploy start --metadata PermissionSet --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1 | tail -20
[Bash] sf project deploy start --metadata PermissionSet --target-org 10xhealth-sean --ignore-conflicts 2>&1 | tail -20
[Bash] sf project deploy report --job-id buyp6asxv --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1 | tail -30

[Bash] cat > /tmp/deploy_perms.txt << 'EOF' Accounting_Basic Accounting_Manager Admin_Team Advisor_Assistant Doctor_Staff Executive Marketing_Manager Marketing_Staff Medical_Coordinator Medical_Staff Nursing_Staff Office_Manager Office_Staff_Basic Operations_Manager Operations_Team PSR_Team Read_Only_Opportunity sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms Shipping_Coordinator SteadyMD_Permission System_Administrator System_Administrator_API_Only Wellness_Assistant Wellness_Manager Wellness_Team EOF  # Create metadata deploy input METADATA_ARGS="" for pset in $(cat /tmp/deploy_perms.txt); do   METADATA_ARGS="${METADATA_ARGS}PermissionSet:${pset} " done  sf project deploy start --metadata $METADATA_ARGS --target-org 10xhealth-sean --ignore-conflicts --wait 30 2>&1 | tail -50

[Bash] cat > /tmp/fix_perms.py << 'EOF' import re import os  pset_files = [     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Accounting_Basic.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Accounting_Manager.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Admin_Team.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Advisor_Assistant.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Doctor_Staff.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Executive.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Marketing_Manager.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Marketing_Staff.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Medical_Coordinator.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Medical_Staff.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Nursing_Staff.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Office_Manager.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Office_Staff_Basic.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Operations_Manager.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Operations_Team.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/PSR_Team.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Read_Only_Opportunity.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/sfdcInternalInt__sfdc_accelerate_dms.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Shipping_Coordinator.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/SteadyMD_Permission.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/System_Administrator.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/System_Administrator_API_Only.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Wellness_Assistant.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Wellness_Manager.permissionset-meta.xml",     "force-app/main/default/permissionsets/Wellness_Team.permissionset-meta.xml", ]  # Fields to remove (that don't exist in Sandbox) invalid_fields = [     "Lead.Blood_Interest__c",     "Account.OperatingHoursId",     "OperatingHours",     "ActiveScratchOrg" ]  for filepath in pset_files:     if not os.path.exists(filepath):         continue          with open(filepath, 'r') as f:         content = f.read()          original_content = content          # Remove invalid field permissions     for invalid_field in invalid_fields:         # Pattern to remove the entire fieldPermissions block for this field         pattern = rf'    <fieldPermissions>.*?<field>{re.escape(invalid_field)}</field>.*?</fieldPermissions>\n'         content = re.sub(pattern, '', content, flags=re.DOTALL)          if original_content != content:         with open(filepath, 'w') as f:             f.write(content)         print(f"CLEANED {filepath}")  EOF python /tmp/fix_perms.py
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata PermissionSet:Admin_Team --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./temp_admin_team 2>&1 | tail -5
[Bash] sf project retrieve start --metadata PermissionSet:Admin_Team --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./temp_admin_team --ignore-conflicts 2>&1 | tail -5
Need Sandbox session to manually add field permissions. Trying to refresh or use SOQL to update permission sets directly. Let me try a programmatic approach using data loader or SOQL instead.

[Bash] cat > /tmp/check_perms_in_sandbox.soql << 'EOF' SELECT Id, Field, PermissionSet.Name, PermissionSetId FROM FieldPermissions  WHERE Field IN ('Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c', 'Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c')  LIMIT 100 EOF  sf data query --query "$(cat /tmp/check_perms_in_sandbox.soql)" --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1

[Bash] sf data query --query "SELECT Id, Field, PermissionSetId FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field IN ('Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c', 'Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c') LIMIT 100" --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1

[Bash] sf data query --query "SELECT Id, Field, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field IN ('Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c', 'Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c') LIMIT 100" --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1
[Bash] sf data query --query "SELECT Id, Field, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c' ORDER BY Parent.Name LIMIT 200" --target-org 10xhealth-sean 2>&1
Creating script to grant field permissions for the new Opportunity fields to all permission sets that have Account_Is_Compliante__c.

[Bash] cat > /tmp/grant_field_perms.py << 'EOF' import subprocess import json  # Get all permission sets that have Account_Is_Compliante__c query = "SELECT ParentId, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c' ORDER BY Parent.Name" result = subprocess.run(     ["sf", "data", "query", "--query", query, "--target-org", "10xhealth-sean", "--json"],     capture_output=True,     text=True )  data = json.loads(result.stdout) perm_sets = {} for record in data.get('records', []):     parent_id = record['ParentId']     parent_name = record['Parent']['Name']     perm_sets[parent_id] = parent_name  print(f"Found {len(perm_sets)} permission sets to update")  # For each permission set, create FieldPermissions for the two new fields new_fields = [     "Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c",     "Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c" ]  count = 0 for parent_id, parent_name in sorted(perm_sets.items()):     for field in new_fields:         # Check if permission already exists         check_query = f"SELECT Id FROM FieldPermissions WHERE ParentId = '{parent_id}' AND Field = '{field}'"         check_result = subprocess.run(             ["sf", "data", "query", "--query", check_query, "--target-org", "10xhealth-sean", "--json"],             capture_output=True,             text=True         )         check_data = json.loads(check_result.stdout)                  if check_data.get('records', []):             print(f"SKIP {parent_name}: {field} already has permission")             continue                  # Create new FieldPermissions record         create_cmd = [             "sf", "data", "create", "record",             "--sobject", "FieldPermissions",             "--values", f"ParentId={parent_id} Field='{field}' PermissionsRead=true PermissionsEdit=false",             "--target-org", "10xhealth-sean",             "--json"         ]                  create_result = subprocess.run(create_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)         create_data = json.loads(create_result.stdout)                  if "Id" in create_data:             print(f"CREATED {parent_name}: {field}")             count += 1         else:             print(f"ERROR {parent_name}: {field} - {create_result.stderr}")  print(f"\nTotal permissions created: {count}") EOF  python /tmp/grant_field_perms.py

[Bash] cat > /tmp/grant_perms.sh << 'EOF' #!/bin/bash  # Get all permission sets that have Account_Is_Compliante__c sf data query \   --query "SELECT ParentId, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c' ORDER BY Parent.Name" \   --target-org 10xhealth-sean \   --csv | tail -n +2 > /tmp/perm_sets.csv  # For each permission set, grant permissions for the new fields count=0 while IFS=',' read -r parent_id parent_name; do   parent_id="${parent_id%\"}" # Remove trailing quote   parent_id="${parent_id#\"}" # Remove leading quote   parent_name="${parent_name%\"}"   parent_name="${parent_name#\"}"      echo "Processing: $parent_name"      # Create field permissions for Account_Billing_State__c   check=$(sf data query --query "SELECT Id FROM FieldPermissions WHERE ParentId = '$parent_id' AND Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c'" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --csv 2>&1 | tail -1)      if [ -z "$check" ] || [[ "$check" == *"No records found"* ]]; then     result=$(sf data create record --sobject FieldPermissions --values "ParentId=$parent_id Field='Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c' PermissionsRead=true PermissionsEdit=false" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --json 2>&1)     if echo "$result" | grep -q '"Id"'; then       echo "  ✓ Created Account_Billing_State__c permission"       ((count++))     fi   fi      # Create field permissions for Account_Main_Practitioner__c   check=$(sf data query --query "SELECT Id FROM FieldPermissions WHERE ParentId = '$parent_id' AND Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c'" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --csv 2>&1 | tail -1)      if [ -z "$check" ] || [[ "$check" == *"No records found"* ]]; then     result=$(sf data create record --sobject FieldPermissions --values "ParentId=$parent_id Field='Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c' PermissionsRead=true PermissionsEdit=false" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --json 2>&1)     if echo "$result" | grep -q '"Id"'; then       echo "  ✓ Created Account_Main_Practitioner__c permission"       ((count++))     fi   fi done < /tmp/perm_sets.csv  echo "Total permissions created: $count" EOF  chmod +x /tmp/grant_perms.sh /tmp/grant_perms.sh 2>&1 | tail -50

[Bash] sf data query \   --query "SELECT ParentId, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c' ORDER BY Parent.Name" \   --target-org 10xhealth-sean \   --json 2>&1 | python3 -c " import sys, json data = json.load(sys.stdin) pset_ids = set() for record in data.get('records', []):     pset_ids.add(record['ParentId']) print('\n'.join(sorted(pset_ids))) " > /tmp/pset_ids.txt  echo "Found $(wc -l < /tmp/pset_ids.txt) permission sets" head -5 /tmp/pset_ids.txt

[PowerShell] $result = & sf data query --query "SELECT ParentId, Parent.Name FROM FieldPermissions WHERE Field = 'Opportunity.Account_Is_Compliante__c' ORDER BY Parent.Name" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --json | ConvertFrom-Json  $psetIds = $result.records | Select-Object -ExpandProperty ParentId -Unique Write-Host "Found $($psetIds.Count) unique permission sets"  # Create field permissions for each one $newFields = @(     "Opportunity.Account_Billing_State__c",     "Opportunity.Account_Main_Practitioner__c" )  $createdCount = 0  foreach($psetId in $psetIds) {     $psetName = ($result.records | Where-Object { $_.ParentId -eq $psetId } | Select-Object -First 1).Parent.Name     Write-Host "Processing: $psetName"          foreach($fieldName in $newFields) {         # Check if permission already exists         $checkResult = & sf data query --query "SELECT Id FROM FieldPermissions WHERE ParentId = '$psetId' AND Field = '$fieldName'" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json                  if ($checkResult.records.Count -eq 0) {             try {                 $createResult = & sf data create record --sobject FieldPermissions --values "ParentId=$psetId Field='$fieldName' PermissionsRead=true PermissionsEdit=false" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --json | ConvertFrom-Json                 if ($createResult.Id) {                     Write-Host "  ✓ Created $fieldName"                     $createdCount++                 }             } catch {                 Write-Host "  ✗ Error creating $fieldName : $_"             }         }     } }  Write-Host "Total permissions created: $createdCount"
